title: The Salt Cathedral
genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
concept: An ocean liner's worth of guilt, exile and wonder, sung from inside a chapel built of salt and weather — a place where confession dissolves the moment it's spoken and the sea keeps every secret anyway.
statement: Built from the same Atlantic DNA as NoiraCiel's earlier sea-soul work, but harder, older, more dangerous — fado's grief stripped of its postcard, replaced with brushed drums, sub bass and a voice that sounds like it's already underwater.
visualIdentity: Submerged stone architecture, salt-crusted iron, rope and brass, candlelight refracted through deep green-black water, a coastline that is more myth than map.
typographyMood: Carved, weathered, slightly eroded — like lettering worn by salt spray. Wide-tracked uppercase for labels, italic serif for confession.
musicalStyle: Atlantic noir, sea-soul, trip-hop jazz, oceanic DnB. Upright bass, brushed/trip-hop drums, deep sub, Portuguese/nylon guitar shimmer, low brass, cello, smoky piano, distant choir, oceanic percussion.
vocalDirection: Crystalline restrained female or androgynous alto, neutral European English, intimate and haunting — close-mic, never belted, like a confession someone wasn't sure they wanted heard.

emotionalArc.phase: Danger and moral discovery at sea
Slow surfacing into wonder and grace
